summary: Soundboard partial with crystal‑clear audio captures a smoking Dark Star → El Paso → Eyes of the World → Morning Dew sequence, showcasing Jerry at his peak.
review: This partial from August 1973 (Garcia’s birthday show) is celebrated for its soaring Dark Star that morphs into a ripping El Paso, an intense Eyes of the World and a heart‑rending Morning Dew. Reviewers repeatedly note the superb playing—Jerry’s slide work, Phil’s bass wizardry—and imply the recording is clean enough to enjoy every nuance.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (13 of 14 with 4‑5 stars) praise both the musical performance and imply a high‑quality soundboard source; only one abusive outlier exists, giving strong confidence in a top‑tier rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No specific audio complaints; reviewers focus on performance and seem to assume clear, balanced sound typical of a soundboard partial.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The partial presents a seamless, high‑energy arc from Dark Star through El Paso into Eyes of the World and culminating in an emotional Morning Dew, highlighted as one of ’73’s finest sequences.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers rave about his peak guitar tone, slide screaming on Dark Star, and a show‑stopping Morning Dew.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for being "plain sick" during the Mind Left Body Jam and overall bass work.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned positively in one review (“I love Bob”) though not highlighted musically.
band_member_comments.Keys: One reviewer angrily insults the keyboards, implying a negative experience with the organ/keys on this recording.
